articles.title: Heatwave, thunderstorms likely across AP, warns APSDMA - The Hans India
articles.full_text:
Amaravati: The Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority has issued a weather alert stating that the State will witness intense heatwave conditions along with thunderstorms accompanied by lightning in some regions on Monday. APSDMA Managing Director Prakhar Jain urged the public to remain cautious.
On Sunday, the highest temperature of 45.4°C was recorded at Kurnool district (Nagaradona). Temperatures above 40°C were recorded in as many as 279 mandals across the State. High temperatures are expected to persist on Monday (April 20), particularly in districts like Palnadu, Prakasam, and Markapur, and Rayalaseema regions.
Severe heatwave conditions are likely to impact 32 mandals across Srikakulam, Vizianagaram, and Parvathipuram Manyam districts, while another 17 mandals may experience moderate heatwave conditions. Several areas recorded extreme temperatures on Sunday, including 44.5°C at Khajipet in Kadapa, 44.4°C at Karapa in Kakinada, and over 43°C in Anantapur, Tirupati, Nellore, and other districts.
The authority advised people to avoid exposure to direct sunlight, especially those with heart conditions, diabetes, or high blood pressure. Citizens are encouraged to stay hydrated by consuming lemon water, buttermilk, coconut water, and drinking water.
Due to trough conditions, light to moderate rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms and lightning is expected in parts of Srikakulam, Vizianagaram, Alluri Sitharama Raju, Polavaram, Kurnool, Nandyal, and Anantapur districts.
Farmers and shepherds have been warned to avoid standing under trees during lightning and to take necessary precautions during sudden weather changes.
